name: Herb Pomeroy
abstract: Irving Herbert Pomeroy III (April 15, 1930 – August 11, 2007) was an American jazz trumpeter, teacher, and the founder of the MIT Festival Jazz Ensemble.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Mel Torme with Herb Pomeroy Big Band and Gerry Mulligan/ Gerry Mulligan Sextet

works.t: Lady, be Good!: Oh, Lady, Be Good!

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: there were a couple of teachers that were very important to me, Herb Pomeroy was one... I think I would have to attribute like a lot of things to Herb and his, not just specific things, but just his inspiration, his confidence building.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: On Thursday nights they had the Herb Pomeroy Big Band. The first time I went in to hear them, I looked and I saw that in the saxophone section, there’s this piano player, Jaki Byard... Herb was a very good leader who later became a fixture at Berklee for many, many, many years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: In fact Herb Pomeroy actually had another baritone player sit there and read the parts and I took all of the solos. But I would look over his shoulder and watch him read and that helped me to learn how to read too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Hal Galper
about_quotes.evidence: So the guy, the head of the department, said, “Why don’t you talk to Herb.” So I called Herb and Herb set me up with what they call a star ... an “Artist’s Diploma”
about_quotes.doc_title: Hal Galper interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 27, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Pat LaBarbera
about_quotes.evidence: all the stuff I had learned at Berklee and then with Herb Pomeroy and Ray Santisi, Charlie Mariano, basically I just retraced my steps and showed him what to do.

about_quotes.speaker: John LaPorta
about_quotes.evidence: I noticed a hundred of them were milling around wondering what to do. There were about 40 of them getting really taken care of by Herb Pomeroy and those people
about_quotes.doc_title: John LaPorta interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 13, 1996
